data.title: Alkami Tech falls on planned $300 mln convertible financing for acquisition
data.description:
** Shares of digital banking firm Alkami Technologies Inc down 6.7% before the bell to $25.48 after acquisition-related capital raise news

** Plano, Texas-based firm offering privately $300 mln 5-yr convertible bonds

** It plans to use portion of net proceeds to pay for capped calls, fund previously announced pending acquisition of MANTL

** On Feb 27, co said it would buy Fin Technologies (MANTL), account opening solution, for $400 mln enterprise value, and announced Q4 results and 2025 outlook

** Co has ~$2.8 bln market cap, per LSEG data

** Through Fri close, shares off ~26% YTD following ~51% gain in 2024

** 8 of 11 analysts covering ALKT rate stock "strong buy" or "buy", rest "hold"; median PT of $45 is 65% above stock's last close - LSEG

data.title: FCC Unit Awarded Contract For Waste Collection In Florida
data.description:
Fomento de Construcciones y Contratas SA :

  • FCC UNIT IN US AWARDED 170 MILLION DOLLAR CONTRACT FOR WASTE COLLECTION IN FLORIDA, U.S.

  • CONTRACT IS EXPECTED TO LAST SEVEN YEARS AND REPRESENTS AN ORDER BOOK VALUE OF 170 MILLION DOLLARS

  • CONTRACT TO BEGIN ON 1ST JANUARY 2026

  • CONTRACT REPRESENTS AN INVESTMENT OF 32 MILLION DOLLARS

data.title: Sector Update: Energy Stocks Lean Lower Pre-Bell Monday
data.description:
Energy stocks leaned lower pre-bell Monday as the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund recently fell by 0.04%.

The United States Oil Fund was up 0.2% and the United States Natural Gas Fund was 4% higher.

Front-month US West Texas Intermediate crude oil was up 0.2% at $67.20 per barrel at the New York Mercantile Exchange. Global benchmark North Sea Brent crude oil gained 0.1% to $70.45 per barrel. Natural gas futures were 3.7% higher at $4.563 per 1 million British Thermal Units.

Veren shares rallied past 20% after the company said it signed a definitive business combination agreement with Whitecap Resources to create a light oil and condensate producer. The all-share transaction is valued at approximately $10.4 billion, inclusive of net debt.

Diversified Energy , FuelCell Energy , and TESIAC said they teamed up to provide up to 360 megawatts of electricity in Virginia, West Virginia, and Kentucky to meet the growing energy needs of data centers. Diversified Energy shares were up over 1% premarket.

Petrobras said it finalized a $283 million agreement to end a legal dispute in the US involving EIG Energy Fund XIV. Shares of Petrobras were down more than 1% pre-bell.

data.title: EU trade chief says U.S. not seeking deal to avert tariffs
data.description:
The Trump administration does not seem to be engaging in talks to avert trade conflict with the European Union, the EU's trade chief said on Monday, two days before the United States is set to impose tariffs on all steel and aluminium imports.

European Trade Commissioner Maros Sefcovic told reporters in Brussels that he had travelled to Washington last month with the aim of starting a dialogue to avoid "the unnecessary pain of measures and countermeasures".

The two sides identified a few areas of mutual benefit to pursue, Sefcovic said.

"But in the end, one hand cannot clap. The U.S. administration does not seem to be engaging to make a deal," he continued.

Sefcovic said that the European Union was ready for constructive engagement, that no one wins from tariffs and that partners with 1.6 trillion euros ($1.7 trillion) of mutual trade should be "extremely careful".

"So our doors are open. But of course, if this would not bring the positive result, in that case we will always protect European businesses, workers and consumers from unjustified tariffs," he said.

"I think we've been very clear about it and I know they expect no less from us."

($1 = 0.9223 euros)

data.title: APA Cut to Outperform From Strong Buy by Raymond James
data.description: (13:21 GMT) APA Price Target Cut to $32.00/Share From $45.00 by Raymond James

data.title: Blade Air Mobility Tests Air Transfer Service Between Downtown Manhattan and JFK Airport
data.description:
Blade Air Mobility said Monday it was partnering with privately-held Skyports Infrastructure to begin a pilot program expected to expand Blade's existing by-the-seat helicopter transfer service between its heliport in downtown Manhattan and JFK International Airport in New York City.

The service is expected to begin next month, the company said. In addition to flying passengers transferring to and from flights at JFK, the new service will also serve people in Queens and Long Island traveling to or from the southern tip of Manhattan near Wall Street, Blade said.

The pilot program is expected to gather data to determine consumer demand, flier experience and logistics specific to the downtown heliport and eventually support the launch of electric vertical aircraft service from the facility, according to the company.

Blade shares were sinking almost 3% in Monday's pre-trade.

data.title: Russians switched to buying FX in February as rouble strengthened sharply
data.description:
A sharp strengthening of the rouble led Russians to switch to making net purchases of foreign currency in February, following five consecutive months of sales, the central bank said in a report on Monday.

The rouble, up around 10.5% in February against the dollar, is gaining this year, mostly on expectations of improved relations between Moscow and Washington that might de-escalate the conflict in Ukraine and facilitate a possible easing of sanctions against Russia.

Russians purchased 6.5 billion roubles ($73.78 million) worth of foreign currency last month, the central bank said in its financial market risks report, after sales worth 32.9 billion roubles in January.

The switch occurred as the rouble sharply strengthened, the bank said, something it attributed to market expectations of a reduction in geopolitical tensions and of a probable decline in the volume of imports.

Net foreign currency sales by major exporters amounted to $12.4 billion, up from $9.9 billion in January, the central bank said.

($1 = 88.0955 roubles)

data.title: TOM Group Widens Loss in 2024
data.description:
TOM Group widened its attributable loss for the year 2024 to HK$256 million from HK$221.4 million in the year-ago period, a Monday filing with the Hong Kong bourse stated.

Loss per share was HK$0.0647, up from HK$0.0559 in the corresponding period of the last fiscal year.

Revenue fell by 4.8% to HK$747 million in the year from HK$784.4 million a year prior, mainly due to an unfavorable foreign exchange rate and challenges related to geopolitical volatility and global economic uncertainties due to high borrowing interest rates.

The Chinese language media company attributed the higher loss mainly to an increase in finance costs in the year.

data.title: Sector Update: Financial Stocks Decline Premarket Monday
data.description:
Financial stocks declined premarket Monday with the Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund 1.3% lower recently.

The Direxion Daily Financial Bull 3X Shares was down almost 4% and its bearish counterpart Direxion Daily Financial Bear 3X Shares was 4.1% higher.

Robinhood Markets has been ordered to pay $3.75 million in restitution to customers and $26 million in fines for violating numerous Financial Industry Regulatory Authority rules, the organization said. Robinhood Markets shares fell past 6.6% pre-bell.

Italian defense and aerospace firm Leonardo replaced Bank of America with Germany's Deutsche Bank in recent weeks to advise on its efforts to create a new European space and satellite entity, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter. Shares of Bank of America were down more than 2%, while Deutsche Bank shares were over 4% lower pre-bell.

Walker & Dunlop shares were down 0.3% after the company priced an amended $450 million senior secured credit agreement.

data.title: Bernstein Trims Stellantis Price Target Amid Ongoing Challenges
data.description:
Bernstein on Monday reduced its price target for Stellantis (STLAM.MI, ) to 11.50 euros from 12.00 euros, reflecting ongoing management and operational challenges the company faces, particularly in North America and Europe.

The European automaker is still without a permanent chief executive officer after Carlos Tavares was ousted from the role in December 2024 amid repeated clashes with existing management.

"After more than halving from its peak level of [Euro]27.19 on 26 March 2024, Stellantis' stock price is now being whipsawed along with its peers by the US tariffs imbroglio with Mexico and Canada. With some 40% of its US sales coming from vehicles manufactured in Mexico or Canada, Stellantis is clearly in the cross-hairs," analysts said, noting near-term challenges for the business. However, recovery is anticipated to be seen from the second half of 2025.

The research firm maintained its market perform rating on the stock.

data.title: Redfin Stock Soars 70% on Deal to Be Bought by Rocket Cos. for $1.75 Billion — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Mackenzie Tatananni

Rocket Cos. said Monday it reached an agreement to buy digital real estate brokerage Redfin.

Rocket will pay $12.50 a share to buy Redfin under the all-stock transaction. The deal values Redfin at $1.75 billion.

Shares of Redfin surged 75% to $10.20 in premarket trading Monday. Rocket was down 11%.

Fintech company Rocket said the deal would connect its mortgage products to Redfin's nearly 50 million monthly visitors.

Redfin was founded in 2004. The company filed a patent the following year for an "online marketplace for real estate transactions." It styled itself as a self-service home search platform, offering users access to local real estate agents and displaying homes for sale on an interactive map.

Rocket also announced Monday that it had agreed to simplify its organizational and capital structure. The company said it would collapse its "Up-C" corporate structure, which is used to take a private company public, and reduce its classes of common stock to two from four.

In connection with the Up-C collapse, Rocket declared a special cash dividend of 80 cents a share of Class A common stock to be paid on April 3.

Write to Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Monday at 9 AM ET
data.description:
U.S. Stock Futures Fall

U.S. stock futures fell, with a new trading week doing little to calm investors' nerves. President Trump over the weekend refused to rule out the U.S. economy entering a recession this year.

German Industrial Production Rose at Start of 2025 as Tariff Threats Loom

Output increased 2% on month in January as German industry showed tentative signs of improvement, though that could be threatened by U.S. trade tariffs.

Made-in-U.S.A. Lumber Futures Are Coming to Wall Street

Southern yellow pine futures will begin trading later this month as prices split from those of Canadian lumber.

What the Dot-Com Bust Can Tell Us About Today's AI Boom

This week marks the 25th anniversary of the Nasdaq peak, but all wasn't lost in the ensuing downturn.

Markets Finally Woke Up to Tariff Reality. Is This a Buying Opportunity?

Your correspondent remains torn, but if you believe in the Trump pitch, now might be a time to take a swing.

How European Defense Stocks Became a Hedge-Fund Home Run

Drastic shifts in the global security environment have sparked a sector rally.

Trump Declines to Rule Out Recession

In an interview that aired Sunday, the president demurred when asked if there were reassurances he could provide to businesses seeking more clarity on tariffs.

Mark Carney Wins Leadership of a Canadian Liberal Party Revived by Trump

The former central banker will officially become Canada's new leader in the coming days and take over his country's response to President Trump's trade war.

Tariff Wars Are Often Short. Their Legacies Aren't.

Economists fear that Trump's tariffs, once they take hold, could have unexpected effects long after he leaves office.

U.S. Dominates Arms Trade but Trump's Moves Risk Rattling Buyers

America has benefited from rising European spending, though the president's move to curb support for Ukraine is raising concerns about future purchases.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Monday at 9 AM ET
data.description:
Beacon Roofing Supply, QXO Discussing Potential Deal

Beacon Roofing Supply and QXO have launched discussions about a potential combination in which Brad Jacobs' company would acquire Beacon for around $7.7 billion.

Rocket to Buy Redfin in $1.75 Billion All-Stock Deal

The mortgage giant Rocket Cos. has agreed to buy Redfin in an all-stock deal valuing the online real-estate brokerage at $1.75 billion.

Whitecap Resources, Veren to Merge in a $10.4 Billion All-Share Deal

Whitecap Resources and Veren have agreed to combine their companies in $10.44 billion deal that will enlarge their Alberta oil and gas footprint.

Foxconn Builds FoxBrain, Its Own AI Model

The world's largest contract electronics maker said that aided by Nvidia it had built a large language model with reasoning capabilities, developed in-house and trained in four weeks.

Report Supports Case for Rio Tinto Unification in Australia, Palliser Says

The activist investor wants Rio Tinto to abandon its primary London listing and unify its corporate structure into a single Australian-domiciled company.

Novo Nordisk Shares Fall After Latest CagriSema Obesity Drug Trial Results

The pharmaceutical giant released fresh trial data showing the drug helped obese or overweight type 2 diabetics lose an average 15.7% of their weight.

7-Eleven Owner, Couche-Tard Financial Advisers Looking for Potential Buyers for Some Stores

Seven & i Holdings said in a letter to shareholders Monday that Couche-Tard recently agreed to jointly explore a potential sale of some stores prior to signing a takeover agreement.

Logistics Startups Are Back in Demand as Companies Navigate Tariffs

Prominent during the pandemic, the startups are now offering software to help companies scenario-plan for a bundle of new tariffs; 'There's definitely a ton of demand,' one CEO says.

CVS's New Mini Stores Go All In on Medicine and Skip Everything Else

The national drugstore chain is preparing to open a dozen stores offering full-service pharmacies but very limited retail.

Bally's Offers Financing Deal to Australia's Star

American betting and entertainment company Bally's has offered to provide embattled casino operator Star Entertainment with additional financing.

data.title: Global Commodities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Commodities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0856 ET - The dollar weakens as markets grapple with recession fears. The ICE's DXY dollar index is on path for its lowest close since early November. The initial euphoria around President Trump's promised market-friendly tax cuts and deregulation gives way to concerns about tariffs possibly boosting inflation, keeping interest rates high and crippling economic growth. Declining Treasury yields, meanwhile, remove a key support for the greenback. The dollar weakens 0.7% versus the yen and around 0.1% versus the euro and the pound. It strengthens 0.3% against the Canadian dollar. U.S. inflation data is due later this week. February CPI is expected to slow a little, in a WSJ survey. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0731 ET - Shares in London-listed miners fell on Chinese deflation fears and the prospect of a recession in the U.S. In European late morning trade, Copper miner Antofagasta was the largest faller. It's shares traded down 3.6% at 1778.50 pence, while Anglo American fell 2.7% to 2317.50 pence. Silver and gold miner Fresnillo fell 1.5% to 836.00 pence, and Glencore fell 0.6% at 317.10 pence. Data showed that China's inflation rate was negative in February and domestic demand was weak, provoking concerns given the threat tariffs could pose to the country's exports, AJ Bell's Russ Mould writes. Separately, President Trump on Sunday refused to rule out a recession in the U.S. this year. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0714 ET - Base metal prices are mixed, with LME three-month copper down 0.7% at $9,536 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum up 0.4% at $2,700 a ton. The global aluminum market is expected to tighten to a 600,000 ton deficit in 2025 on falling supply, JPMorgan analysts say in a note. LME aluminum prices need to rise to $2,850 a ton or more in order to fully incentivize supply growth from production restarts, JPM says. Around 475,000 tons of restarted production is needed in Europe over the coming two years to limit--but not alleviate--projected aluminum deficits, analysts write. Even with lower input production costs expected later in 2025, deficit fundamentals continue to point towards aluminum prices needing to reach $2,850 a ton in the fourth quarter in order to restart idled production, JPM adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0618 ET - Palm oil ended lower amid data showing weaker exports. Malaysia's palm oil exports fell 16% on month to 1.00 million metric tons in February, according to the Malaysian Palm Oil Board. Weak soybean oil prices also contributed to the decline in CPO prices, said David Ng, a trader at Kuala Lumpur-based Iceberg X. Ng sees support for CPO prices at 4,450 ringgit/ton and resistance at 4,700 ringgit/ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery is 123 ringgit lower at 4,502 ringgit/ton.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0519 ET - Gold futures are broadly flat in relatively muted trading. Futures are flat on $2,912.10 a troy ounce. The precious metal sits up 0.4% on week on lingering safe-haven demand and a weaker U.S. dollar. That said, gold is trading in a tight range as markets appear to be pricing out the inflationary effect of president Trump's tariff policies given constant reversals, Sucden Financial's Daria Efanova says in a note. Market attention is fixed on Wednesday's U.S. Consumer Price Index data, and Producer Price Index data set for release on Thursday. The economic data could provide more clues as to the Federal Reserve's pathway to monetary policy easing. Lower interest rates typically raise the appeal of non-interest bearing bullion. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

0308 ET - Comex gold futures are undergoing consolidation, based on the daily chart, RHB Retail Research's Joseph Chai says in a research report. The commodity saw a new "lower low" close on Friday, with its relative strength index rounding downwards, which suggests that bullish momentum is taking a longer pause, he says. The precious metal could shift to a sideways movement above the $2,850/oz support level. Meanwhile, the 50-day simple moving average is trending upward, providing support to the bullish technical setup. As long as the commodity stays above $2,800/oz, the technical setup is perceived bullish, the analyst adds. Spot gold is 0.2% higher at $2,914.26/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0145 ET - The physical bullion short-squeeze is poised to send gold above $3,000/oz, says Heng Koon How of UOB's Global Economics & Markets Research in a note. "Trade tariff uncertainties have resulted in a strong and sudden shipment of gold bullion back into COMEX and the U.S., triggering a global short squeeze in gold," says the head of Markets Strategy. Also, current key positive drivers for gold remain intact, such as increasing concerns over a growth slowdown, he adds. UOB recently raised its gold forecasts to $2,900/oz from $2,800/oz for 2Q, $3,000/oz from $2,900/oz for 3Q, and to $3,100/oz from $3,000 for 4Q. Spot gold is little changed at $2,908.57/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2241 ET - Palm oil prices rise in Asian trading, amid concerns over slower production in Malaysia's key growing areas, AmInvestment Bank says in a note. Expectations of stronger demand from India are also supporting prices, it adds. Malaysian Palm Oil Board's February supply and demand data are due afternoon. Technical analysis suggests CPO futures appear bullish, and traders may consider initiating long positions if prices adjust, it adds. AmInvestment Bank sees support for CPO prices at 4,525 ringgit/ton and resistance at 4,688 ringgit/ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery rises 26 ringgit to 4,651 ringgit/ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

2230 ET - Base metals are broadly lower in early Asian trading, with the three-month LME copper contract down 0.3% at $9,585.00 a ton, aluminum 0.2% lower and nickel off 0.45%. Markets are weighing the impact of duties on U.S. imports of aluminum and steel, as well as the possibility of tariffs on copper, ANZ Research writes in a note. Also, expectations for Chinese stimulus measures have risen after China's CPI declined more than expected in February, it adds. Tariffs could put upward pressure on copper spot prices, given the commodity's relatively low global inventory, though sharp shifts in U.S. trade and foreign policy will likely cause further disruption to metal markets, the research team says in a separate note. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

2228 ET - Iron ore rises in early Asian trade. Prices fell last week as the market considers cuts to Chinese steel production, ANZ Research analysts write in a note. During the National People's Congress, China's economic planning agency announced that it would mandate steel production cuts, they say. However, losses were limited after the Shanghai Metal Markets reported that iron ore port inventory fell significantly last week, the analysts add. The most-traded iron-ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is up 0.45% at 778.0 yuan a ton.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

2000 ET - Gold edges higher in the early morning Asian session, supported by prospects of a U.S. recession that could enhance the safe-haven appeal of the precious metal. In an interview with Fox News on Sunday, President Trump refused to rule out a recession for the U.S. this year. "Traders and investors are reacting to a sharp and sudden deterioration in U.S. economic data," Saxo Bank's Ole S. Hansen says in a research report. Gold's outlook is still supportive, especially given the limited scope of its latest correction, which signals strong demand despite selling pressure from technically focused traders, the head of commodity strategy says. Spot gold is 0.2% higher at $2,915.98/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

1923 ET - Synlait Milk's turnaround has been quicker than Bell Potter analyst Jonathan Snape anticipated, but question marks remain over the future of the dairy producer's advanced nutrition business. Snape tells clients that the pace of the turnaround has been reflected by a rally in Synlait's shares. He calls out the improvement in Synlait's ingredients business, continued cost control, and new business development within advanced nutrition. However, he warns that the latter could take a hit if a2 Milk, which had an exclusivity agreement with Synlait until January 2025, vertically integrates into supply chain assets. Bell Potter raises its target price to A$0.90 from A$0.425, and maintains a hold rating. Shares are up 2.5% at A$0.83. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Greenland Hong Kong Logs 457 Million Yuan in Contracted Sales for Two Months to February
data.description: Greenland Hong Kong Holdings recorded contracted sales of 457 million yuan and a contracted gross floor area of 79,044 square meters in the two months to February, a Monday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

data.title: Oil Prices Rise Despite China's Weakening Economy and Trump's Tariff Threats
data.description:
Oil rose for a third day on Monday even as China's economy weakened in February and U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff threats continue to roil markets.

West Texas Intermediate crude oil for April delivery was last seen up US$0.33 to US$67.37 per barrel, while May Brent crude rose US$0.32 to US$70.68.

The rise comes as China reported consumer prices weakened last month, while wholesale prices also dropped. The country is the No.1 oil importer but its demand growth has stalled as its key real-estate sector deals with a debt crisis and consumer spending falls.

China's "National Bureau of Statistics Consumer Price Index fell 0.7% last month year-on-year and 0.2% month-on-month against a 0.7% rise in January. The Producer Price Index has now fallen every month since September 2022 as overcapacity and weak consumption continues deflation or stagflation bannering by many economists", PVM Oil Associates noted.

Rising oil prices also come as U.S. trade policies continue to unsettle traders. President Donald Trump continues to threaten to impose tariffs on the U.S.'s largest trading partners even as he delays the imposition of the levies. Trump last week postponed tariffs on imports covered by a free-trade agreement with Mexico and Canada to April 2, while doubling the tariffs on Chinese imports to 20%.

data.title: Kelfred Expects Swing to Loss in 2024
data.description:
Kelfred Holdings expects a net loss of HK$21.6 million for 2024, against a net profit of HK$1.4 million from 2023, a Monday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The eyewear products company attributed the expected swing to a reduction in gross profit margins due to a decrease in the average selling price of eyewear products, a decrease in exchange gains, and a lack of demolition compensation compared to 2023.

data.description:
Microstrategy Inc :

  • MICROSTRATEGY FILES FOR OFFERING OF UP TO $21 BILLION OF 8.00% SERIES A PERPETUAL STRIKE PREFERRED STOCK - SEC FILING

data.title: China's Fiscal Support Package to Amplify Rising Debt, Fitch Says
data.description:
China's planned fiscal stimulus for 2025 will anchor economic growth but also result in a notable budget deficit that will add to rising government debt, Fitch Ratings said in a Friday release.

Escalating public debt was a main driver for Fitch's April 2024 revision of China's A+ sovereign rating outlook to negative.

Fitch projects China's budgeted fiscal deficit rising to 8.8% of GDP in 2025, higher than the 6.5% recorded last year.

The substantial deficit was due to a revenue drop that included a structural decrease in property-related revenue.

The rating agency sees continued growth in general government debt, potentially exceeding 60% of GDP in 2025.

Fitch expects further monetary easing and potential renminbi depreciation to aid in countering the effects of US tariffs.

Despite an ambitious growth goal of 5%, Fitch estimates China's real GDP growth to reach 4.3% in 2025, driven by local demand constraints, property sector challenges, and external pressures.

data.title: Concentra Acquires Assets of Physicians Health Center, OM Management
data.description:
Concentra Group said Monday it has acquired the assets of Physicians Health Center and OM Management.

Financial terms weren't disclosed.

The occupational health services provider will offer work injury care, physical therapy, drug testing, DOT physical exams, pre-placement exams, and other employer-related health services from its new physicians health center locations in Miami, it said, adding that the OM Management corporate office will become a satellite office for the company's CMCA group.
